title: NeuroMatch

description.paragraph1: A company created a dating app that uses brain scanning to find 'perfect emotional compatibility'.
description.paragraph2: Users access the system through various devices: phones, neural glasses, holographic consoles, and even smart mirrors.
description.paragraph3: All heavy processing happens on a giant central server called CUP1D0.
description.paragraph4: The problem is that when the server went down for 11 minutes, thousands of couples had their memories synchronized with random people.

bullet_point.bullet1: The system needs to work on [b]extremely different devices[/b].
bullet_point.bullet2: If the main server fails, practically everything stops working.
bullet_point.bullet3: Must [b]prevent intruders[/b] from accessing other users' personal information.

footnote: [i]~ The divorce rate increased by 3000% this month, I wonder why?[/i]

bug1.description: During an update, the team decided to shut down the CUP1D0 server without redundancy. Immediately all applications became unusable and users started losing emotional synchronizations. Which classic weakness of Client-Server architecture caused this?
